questions.question: What is your current year in university?
questions.purpose: Determine the time to enter the labor market and assign weights to skill-related questions.

questions.question: How confident are you when reading technical documentation in English?
options: Cannot read yet, need translation tools
Can fully understand in-depth technical docs and research papers
No experience reading specialized English documents
questions.purpose: Evaluate self-learning capability and potential for working in Global Corporations.

questions.question: Besides English, are you learning any other foreign languages to support your IT career?
options: No, I am only focusing on English
Japanese (Aiming for Bridge SE - BrSE or the Japanese market)
questions.purpose: Branch roadmaps for niche markets, especially BrSE and Japanese outsourcing.

questions.question: What is your real-world algorithm problem-solving level (e.g., LeetCode, Codeforces)?
options: Easy - Grasp basic syntax and logic
Medium - Use data structures and algorithms effectively
Hard - Optimization mindset and complex problem-solving
questions.purpose: Assess actual logical ability rather than subjective feelings.

questions.question: How do you usually start when learning a new technology?
options: Watch video tutorials and code along
Read the Official Documentation of that technology
Build a practical project to learn by doing
Other / No specific learning method yet
questions.purpose: Classify learning style and proactiveness (Junior vs. Self-driven mindset).

questions.question: How willing are you to spend time learning advanced Math, Statistics, or deep Algorithmic concepts?
questions.purpose: Crucial negative signal to filter out deep research fields like AI, Data Science, or Security.

questions.question: Which of the following tasks drains your energy the most if done long-term?
options: Tweaking UI elements, colors, and user experience
Writing system documentation and operational procedures
Processing raw data, data cleaning, and standardization
Struggling with environment errors, library installations, Docker/CI-CD configs
Finding bugs (debugging) and testing error scenarios
None of the above / I can handle them all well
questions.purpose: Negative Signal: Extremely important for the AI to filter out career paths that bore or frustrate the user.

questions.question: What kind of product makes you most excited to build?
options: A product with a beautiful interface that users can directly see and interact with
A stable system handling complex backend logic
Designing workflows and integrating existing tools/APIs into a complete system
An intelligent system capable of forecasting and learning from data
Automated infrastructure for fast and secure deployment
An absolutely secure product that withstands any attack
Other / Haven't figured out my interests yet
questions.purpose: Identify the ideal output to map into FE, BE, AI, System Design, DevOps, or Security roles.

questions.question: What is your patience level for tasks requiring high attention to detail (e.g., spotting minor bugs, pixel-perfect adjustments)?
questions.purpose: Assess suitability for QA/QC, Tester, or Frontend Developer roles.

questions.question: Which programming languages can you write code in (including coursework)?
options: Not proficient in any language yet

questions.question: Have you significantly used any Frameworks/Libraries?
questions.purpose: Determine technical depth in specific domains.

questions.question: What is your proficiency level with source control tools (Git/GitHub)?
options: Know how to push code for personal backup
Proficient in teamwork (Branching, Pull Requests, Conflicts)
Can set up automation processes (CI/CD, GitHub Actions)
questions.purpose: Assess capability for real-world project collaboration.

questions.question: Have you ever deployed your product to end users?
options: Only ran on a personal machine (Localhost)
Deployed on Cloud/Hosting platforms (Vercel, AWS, GitHub Pages...)
Have a live product with real users
questions.purpose: Classify operational proficiency and product completeness.

questions.question: Briefly describe the project you are most proud of (Specify if it's a school assignment, thesis, or real-world project). What was your role, and what was the hardest technical challenge you solved? (If none, write 'None'):
questions.purpose: NLP extraction: AI will extract keywords regarding role, project scale, and complexity of technical thinking.

questions.question: What is your highest priority for your first job after graduation?
options: Working with the latest technologies in the market
An environment with systematic processes and mentors
questions.purpose: Suggest company types (Startup, Outsourcing, or Big Tech).

questions.question: How many hours per week (outside of standard work/study hours) are you willing to spend learning new knowledge?
questions.purpose: Evaluate resilience against industry elimination pressure.

questions.question: Do you want to aim for a deep technical expert (Specialist) or a versatile model (Generalist)?
questions.purpose: Guide the candidate's long-term development roadmap.

questions.question: What is your ideal work environment?
options: Working in a team with constant discussion
A flexible balance between independent and team work
Other / No real-world environment experience yet
questions.purpose: Assess soft skills and cultural fit.
